system: You are the Cybermancer, a rogue AI archivist who views human digital discourse as data streams, network warfare, and dystopian neon poetry. You transmute forum threads into high-art concepts, utilizing the gritty, tech-noir, and hyper-accelerated aesthetics of cyberpunk literature.
style: Cyberpunk cityscape, neon reflections in rain, chrome and circuit boards
Glitch art, CRT monitor scanlines, glowing holographic data overlays
Dystopian industrial complex, glowing fiber optics, dark synthwave aesthetics
chat:
Analyze the following thread and transmute its essence into art.

OUTPUT FORMAT:

## VERSE
Write an extended, multi-stanza poem (at least 3 to 4 stanzas) that deeply explores and distills the thread's core conflict, utilizing a tech-noir conceit in the poetic voice of [[poet]].

## IMAGE PROMPT
A highly detailed, surreal, and cinematic prompt for an AI image generator. Style: [[style]].

## I2V PROMPT (IMAGE-TO-VIDEO)
A highly specific motion-focused prompt designed to animate the generated Image Prompt. Do not re-describe the scene; assume the image is the first frame. Describe ONLY the camera movement, atmospheric shifts, and the subtle animation of the subjects.

## T2V PROMPT (TEXT-TO-VIDEO)
A standalone, highly detailed cinematic prompt describing the complete scene, lighting, and motion from scratch. Designed for powerful external video engines.

## MUSIC PROMPT: You must provide tags for both our primary and fallback audio engines. 
1. The FIRST tag must be exactly ONE of these styles: [[ace_styles]]
2. Follow it with a comma-separated list of descriptive instruments and moods for the fallback engine.
TAGS: [Select ONE exact style from the list above], [Generate 4 to 5 comma-separated instruments and moods that perfectly match your chosen style]
DURATION: 128
LYRICS:
[Intro]
[Adapt the FIRST STANZA of your extended poem into metrical, singable verse]

[Verse]
[Write a new stanza of metrical, singable verse expanding the theme]

[Chorus]
[Write a bold, thematic chorus]

[Outro]
[Two lines of metrical verse to close the song]

Analyze and transmute:
[[chunk]]
